verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted trypsin-like serine protease with no classical transmembrane domain. However, multiple peer-reviewed studies (Shen et al., Clin Cancer Res 2025) and Phase I clinical data confirm that KLK2 is expressed on the cell surface of AR+ prostate cancer cells, validated by IHC, multiplex immunofluorescence, EpCAM co-localization, and direct antibody binding/internalization assays. This surface expression is restricted to AR-active prostate epithelial/cancer cells and is not observed in KLK2-negative cell lines. Active clinical programs (pasritamig KLK2×CD3 bispecific now in Phase 3, CAR-T, 225Ac-RLT) depend on this surface-accessible pool. Verdict: contextual/cell_state_induced — surface only in AR+ prostate cancer context.
